comment.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+import { defineComponent } from 'vue'
+import type { DefineSetupFnComponent, SlotsType, VNode } from 'vue'
+import { useState } from '../composables/state'
+
+interface PreviewState {
+  enabled: boolean
+  state: Record<any, unknown>
+  _initialized?: boolean
+}
+
+type PreviewOnlySlots = SlotsType<{
+  default?: () => VNode[]
+  /**
+   * If you require a replacement when preview mode is not enabled.
+   */
+  fallback?: () => VNode[]
+}>
+
+const PreviewOnly = defineComponent({
+  name: 'PreviewOnly',
+  inheritAttrs: false,
+  ...(import.meta.dev && {
+    slots: Object as PreviewOnlySlots,
+  }),
+  setup (_, props) {
+    const preview = useState<PreviewState>('_preview-state', () => ({
+      enabled: false,
+      state: {},
+    }))
+
+    return () => preview.value.enabled ? props.slots.default?.() : props.slots.fallback?.()
+  },
+}) as unknown as DefineSetupFnComponent<{}, {}, PreviewOnlySlots>
comment.body: That's the current behavior, I'm just wondering if making it only async would lighten a bit the bundle for end-users.

issue.title: [BUG]: Docs -> GH action integration is getting flagged by zizmor itself
issue.body:
### Pre-submission checks

- [x] I am **not** filing a feature request. These should be filed via the feature request form instead.
- [x] I have checked the [Troubleshooting Guide](https://docs.zizmor.sh/troubleshooting) for my problem.
- [x] I have looked through both the [open](https://github.com/zizmorcore/zizmor/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+) and [closed](https://github.com/zizmorcore/zizmor/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20state%3Aclosed) issues for a duplicate report.

### zizmor version

1.25.2

### Expected behavior

Hello there :wave: 

First. A huge thank you for this amazing project that helped me a lot and many other OSS projects :) 

https://docs.zizmor.sh/integrations/#manual-integration

the provided snippets shouldn't be flagged by zizmor itself (except if it's on purpose ?)



### Actual behavior

astral-sh/uv seems to enable caching by default and zizmor flag this.



### Reproduction steps

1. use the snippet for manual integration with advanced security
2. zizmor flag its own yml file

### Logs

```plain text

```

### Additional context

_No response_

expected. if there's any error, we'd consider it as a corrupted package and ignore it
this is also getting cached :/

issue.title: [Bug]: @storybook/tanstack-react doesnt support pathless route group directories
issue.body:
### Describe the bug

The new tanstack-react framework does not work with any route underneath a pathless group directory. I believe this is because the path does not match the id (which is the case for all other routes)

https://tanstack.com/router/latest/docs/routing/routing-concepts#pathless-route-group-directories

### Reproduction link

https://github.com/hollandjake/github-cmewxfni

### Reproduction steps

1. Run storybooks
2. Open the Page example story
3. Note the error "Route not found:"

### System

```bash
│
│  
│  Storybook Environment Info:
│

│  System:
│  OS: macOS 26.5
│  CPU: (12) arm64 Apple M4 Pro
│  Shell: 5.9 - /bin/zsh
│  Binaries:
│  Node: 22.15.0 - /Users/jholland/.nvm/versions/node/v22.15.0/bin/node

│  npm: 11.15.0 - /Users/jholland/.nvm/versions/node/v22.15.0/bin/npm <----- active
```

_No response_

comment.body:
Sorry about the long delay, was pretty packed at work. 

From the documentation, it seems like cache is enabled by default on GH hosted runners. Did you run the CI on self-hosted runners ? 

```
  # Enable uploading of the uv cache: true, false, or auto (enabled on GitHub-hosted runners, disabled on self-hosted runners)
    enable-cache: "auto"

    # Glob pattern to match files relative to the repository root to control the cache
    cache-dependency-glob: |
      **/*requirements*.txt
      **/*requirements*.in
      **/*constraints*.txt
      **/*constraints*.in
      **/pyproject.toml
      **/uv.lock
      **/*.py.lock

    # Whether to restore the cache if found
    restore-cache: "true"

    # Whether to save the cache after the run
    save-cache: "true"
```

https://github.com/astral-sh/setup-uv
https://github.com/storybookjs/mcp/pull/259

When trying to test a component that uses a tanstack hook as per https://storybook.js.org/docs/get-started/frameworks/tanstack-react#using-router-parameters-with-a-non-route-component

It errors with `Route cannot have both an 'id' and a 'path' option`

```
parameters: {
```

issue.title: TanStack: Normalize route-group handling in createRoute mock

labels.name: ci:normal
labels.description: Run our default set of CI jobs (choose this for most PRs).

labels.name: qa:skip
labels.description: Pull Requests that do not need any QA.

issue.body:
`@storybook/tanstack-react` failed to render stories under TanStack pathless route-group directories because mocked file routes used the raw file-route id as the navigable path. For routes like `/(group)/page`, this produced invalid runtime path matching and surfaced `Route not found`.

- **Route path normalization in router mock**
  - Updated `createFileRoute` in `src/export-mocks/react-router.ts` to normalize `path/fullPath` by removing pathless group segments (e.g. `/(group)`), while preserving the original route `id` for lookup/override behavior.

- **Behavior preserved where identity matters**
  - Route identity remains file-based (`id` unchanged), so existing id-keyed logic (e.g. route overrides) continues to target the same route nodes.

- **Regression coverage**
  - Added focused tests in `src/export-mocks/react-router.test.ts` for:
    - grouped leaf route (`/(group)/page` → path `/page`)
    - pure group route (`/(group)` → path `/`)
  - Added `code/frameworks/tanstack-react/vitest.config.ts` to run package-local tests consistently.

```ts
// before: id/path/fullPath all used raw file path
createFileRoute('/(group)/page') // path mismatch at runtime

// after: keep id, normalize navigable path
// id: '/(group)/page'
// path/fullPath: '/page'
```

#### Manual testing

- covered by the tests 

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Route paths now normalize group and layout notation, strip extraneous underscore markers, and default empty results to "/" for consistent URLs.
  * Initial route path inference improved to prefer normalized identifiers before falling back to child/full-path or root.

* **Tests**
  * Added tests validating route ID preservation and path/fullPath normalization across grouped and layouted routes.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

issue.title: [Bug]: @storybook/tanstack-react root layout not working
issue.body: ### Describe the bug First of all, thank you so much for the tanstack-router integration! ## What I am doing I am trying to use `@storybook/tanstack-react` to render TanStack Router file routes inside their real app route hierarchy. I have... - `routes/__root.tsx` using `createRootRoute` and rendering a root layout with ``. - `routes/index.tsx` using `createFileRoute("/")` and rendering the home page content. And I am trying to create a story that shows the home page in the same way the real app shows it (with the root layout and the home page content inside the outlet). Unfortunately, I can only render either the root layout or the page content, but not both. Here is a minimal reproduction repo: https://github.com/infused-kim/tmp-storybook (It was forked from another bug report repo and modified to show my issue.) ## What I am expecting to happen I am expecting to see both the root layout and the page content in the story (like the real app): ## What is actually happening I added three stories to demonstrate the issue: ### `src/stories/01-leaf-route-only.stories.ts` This story uses `route: Route` from `src/routes/index.tsx` and it shows _only_ the page route content. The root layout is not included. ### `src/stories/02-leaf-route-after-route-tree-import.stories.ts` This story imports `src/routeTree.gen.ts` first, then uses `route: Route` and it shows _only_ the root layout. The page route content is not included. This happens as soon as ANY story imports `src/routeTree.gen.ts`. So it's named `.stories-disabled.ts` to allow the first example to work. ### `src/stories/03-route-tree-with-path.stories-disabled.ts` This story imports the routeTree and passes it as the route with the path `/`. It shows _only_ the root layout. The page route content is not included (same as 02). ## Suspected cause In `src/routes/__root.tsx`, I added: ```ts console.log('[storybook-tanstack-repro] __root Outlet source:', String(Outlet)); ``` In Storybook, this logs that `Outlet` is: ```ts () => null ``` This appears to come from the `@storybook/tanstack-react` mock for `@tanstack/react-router`, which exports a mocked `Outlet` that returns `null`. That means the root layout renders, but its `` cannot render the matched child route, so the nested page content disappears. ## Environment - OS: macOS 26.5 (25F71) - Node.js: v25.8.2 - pnpm: 10.33.4 Packages from the reproduction repo: - `storybook`: 10.4.1 - `@storybook/tanstack-react`: 10.4.1 - `@storybook/addon-a11y`: 10.4.1 - `@storybook/addon-docs`: 10.4.1 - `@storybook/addon-vitest`: 10.4.1 - `@tanstack/react-router`: 1.170.10 - `@tanstack/react-start`: 1.168.18 - `@tanstack/router-plugin`: 1.168.13 - `@vitejs/plugin-react`: 6.0.2 - `vite`: 8.0.14 - `typescript`: 6.0.3 - `react`: 19.2.6 - `react-dom`: 19.2.6 ## Other thoughts ### Potential documentation improvement In this section: https://storybook.js.org/docs/get-started/frameworks/tanstack-react#rendering-nested-routes > When route is a file route connected to your app's route tree, Storybook automatically includes parent layout routes so the story renders inside the same nested hierarchy as the real app. You can also pass the routeTree export from routeTree.gen.ts directly. But there is no example of passing the routeTree to `route`. It's also confusing what `connected to your app's route tree` means. ### Automatic routeTree loading It seems like storybook doesn't load the routeTree automatically and requires the user to import it first. That's a bit unintuitive and perhaps storybook could read the `generatedRouteTree` option the vite config and automatically import it. ### Reproduction link https://github.com/infused-kim/tmp-storybook ### Reproduction steps 1. Launch storybook in the reproduction repo 2. Check the story `Repro/01 Leaf Route Only`. - You will see route content without the layout 3. Rename `02-leaf-route-after-route-tree-import.stories-disabled.ts` to remove `-disabled` 4. Check the story `Repro/02 Leaf Route After RouteTree Import` - You will see layout content without the route content - Since this story imports the routeTree, `Repro/01 Leaf Route Only` is now also showing the layout and not the content 5. Rename `03-route-tree-with-path.stories-disabled.ts` to remove `-disabled` 6. Check the story `Repro/03 RouteTree With Path` - You will see layout content without the route content ### System ```bash │ Storybook Environment Info: │ │ System: │ OS: macOS 26.5 │ CPU: (8) arm64 Apple M1 │ Shell: 4.5.0 - /opt/homebrew/bin/fish │ Binaries: │ Node: 24.16.0 - │ /Users/Kim/.local/state/fnm_multishells/10315_1779067619764/bin/node │ Yarn: 1.22.22 - │ /Users/Kim/.local/state/fnm_multishells/10315_1779067619764/bin/yarn │ npm: 11.13.0 - │ /Users/Kim/.local/state/fnm_multishells/10315_1779067619764/bin/npm │ pnpm: 11.5.0 - │ /Users/Kim/.local/state/fnm_multishells/10315_1779067619764/bin/pnpm <----- │ active │ Browsers: │ Chrome: 148.0.7778.215 │ Firefox: 147.0.4 │ Safari: 26.5 │ npmPackages: │ @storybook/addon-a11y: ^10.4.1 => 10.4.1 │ @storybook/addon-docs: ^10.4.1 => 10.4.1 │ @storybook/addon-mcp: ^0.6.0 => 0.6.0 │ @storybook/addon-vitest: ^10.4.1 => 10.4.1 │ @storybook/tanstack-react: ^10.4.1 => 10.4.1 │ storybook: ^10.4.1 => 10.4.1 ``` ### Additional context _No response_

issue.title: Add a PreviewOnly component

labels.name: 🍰 p2-nice-to-have
labels.description: Nice to have, not blocking

type.name: Enhancement
type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality

issue.body:
### Describe the feature

Nuxt already provides `<DevOnly>` as a small built-in wrapper for development-only UI. I think a similar `<PreviewOnly>` component would be a useful addition for preview-gated UI built on top of Nuxt preview mode.

The idea is intentionally small: render the default slot when preview mode is enabled, and optionally render a `#fallback` slot otherwise. This would not behave like `<DevOnly>` at build time; preview mode is runtime-controlled, so the component would just be a convenience wrapper around the preview state.

I opened a small PR with one possible implementation so there is something concrete to discuss: #35192.

### Additional information

- [x] Would you be willing to help implement this feature?
- [ ] Could this feature be implemented as a module?

### Final checks

- [x] Read the [contribution guide](https://nuxt.com/docs/4.x/community/contribution).
- [x] Check existing [discussions](https://github.com/nuxt/nuxt/discussions) and [issues](https://github.com/nuxt/nuxt/issues).

labels.name: ✨ enhancement
labels.description: New feature or improvement to existing functionality

labels.name: 🍰 p2-nice-to-have
labels.description: Nice to have, not blocking

The root layout is not included. ### `src/stories/02-leaf-route-after-route-tree-import.stories.ts` This story imports `src/routeTree.gen.ts` first, then uses `route: Route` and it shows _only_ the root layout. The page route content is not included. This happens as soon as ANY story imports `src/routeTree.gen.ts`. So it's named `.stories-disabled.ts` to allow the first example to work. ### `src/stories/03-route-tree-with-path.stories-disabled.ts` This story imports the routeTree and passes it as the route with the path `/`. It shows _only_ the root layout. The page route content is not included (same as 02). ## Suspected cause In `src/routes/__root.tsx`, I added: ```ts console.log('[storybook-tanstack-repro] __root Outlet source:', String(Outlet)); ``` In Storybook, this logs that `Outlet` is: ```ts () => null ``` This appears to come from the `@storybook/tanstack-react` mock for `@tanstack/react-router`, which exports a mocked `Outlet` that returns `null`. That means the root layout renders, but its `` cannot render the matched child route, so the nested page content disappears. ## Environment - OS: macOS 26.5 (25F71) - Node.js: v25.8.2 - pnpm: 10.33.4 Packages from the reproduction repo: - `storybook`: 10.4.1 - `@storybook/tanstack-react`: 10.4.1 - `@storybook/addon-a11y`: 10.4.1 - `@storybook/addon-docs`: 10.4.1 - `@storybook/addon-vitest`: 10.4.1 - `@tanstack/react-router`: 1.170.10 - `@tanstack/react-start`: 1.168.18 - `@tanstack/router-plugin`: 1.168.13 - `@vitejs/plugin-react`: 6.0.2 - `vite`: 8.0.14 - `typescript`: 6.0.3 - `react`: 19.2.6 - `react-dom`: 19.2.6 ## Other thoughts ### Potential documentation improvement In this section: https://storybook.js.org/docs/get-started/frameworks/tanstack-react#rendering-nested-routes > When route is a file route connected to your app's route tree, Storybook automatically includes parent layout routes so the story renders inside the same nested hierarchy as the real app. You can also pass the routeTree export from routeTree.gen.ts directly. But there is no example of passing the routeTree to `route`. It's also confusing what `connected to your app's route tree` means. ### Automatic routeTree loading It seems like storybook doesn't load the routeTree automatically and requires the user to import it first. That's a bit unintuitive and perhaps storybook could read the `generatedRouteTree` option the vite config and automatically import it. ### Reproduction link https://github.com/infused-kim/tmp-storybook ### Reproduction steps 1. Launch storybook in the reproduction repo 2. Check the story `Repro/01 Leaf Route Only`. - You will see route content without the layout 3. Rename `02-leaf-route-after-route-tree-import.stories-disabled.ts` to remove `-disabled` 4. Check the story `Repro/02 Leaf Route After RouteTree Import` - You will see layout content without the route content - Since this story imports the routeTree, `Repro/01 Leaf Route Only` is now also showing the layout and not the content 5. Rename `03-route-tree-with-path.stories-disabled.ts` to remove `-disabled` 6. Check the story `Repro/03 RouteTree With Path` - You will see layout content without the route content ### System ```bash │ Storybook Environment Info: │ │ System: │ OS: macOS 26.5 │ CPU: (8) arm64 Apple M1 │ Shell: 4.5.0 - /opt/homebrew/bin/fish │ Binaries: │ Node: 24.16.0 - │ /Users/Kim/.local/state/fnm_multishells/10315_1779067619764/bin/node │ Yarn: 1.22.22 - │ /Users/Kim/.local/state/fnm_multishells/10315_1779067619764/bin/yarn │ npm: 11.13.0 - │ /Users/Kim/.local/state/fnm_multishells/10315_1779067619764/bin/npm │ pnpm: 11.5.0 - │ /Users/Kim/.local/state/fnm_multishells/10315_1779067619764/bin/pnpm <----- │ active │ Browsers: │ Chrome: 148.0.7778.215 │ Firefox: 147.0.4 │ Safari: 26.5 │ npmPackages: │ @storybook/addon-a11y: ^10.4.1 => 10.4.1 │ @storybook/addon-docs: ^10.4.1 => 10.4.1 │ @storybook/addon-mcp: ^0.6.0 => 0.6.0 │ @storybook/addon-vitest: ^10.4.1 => 10.4.1 │ @storybook/tanstack-react: ^10.4.1 => 10.4.1 │ storybook: ^10.4.1 => 10.4.1 ``` ### Additional context _No response_

comment.body:
Hey @infused-kim  :wave: ,

Thank you for your issue and kind words, very great reproduction BTW :) . 

If you think documentations can get improved, feel free to open a PR !

Regarding `Automatic routeTree loading`, that was done on purpose to keep stories lighter and faster but you can set it as default through `preview.ts`.

issue.title: TanStack: Normalize route-group handling in createRoute mock

labels.name: ci:normal
labels.description: Run our default set of CI jobs (choose this for most PRs).

labels.name: qa:skip
labels.description: Pull Requests that do not need any QA.

issue.body:
`@storybook/tanstack-react` failed to render stories under TanStack pathless route-group directories because mocked file routes used the raw file-route id as the navigable path. For routes like `/(group)/page`, this produced invalid runtime path matching and surfaced `Route not found`.

- **Route path normalization in router mock**
  - Updated `createFileRoute` in `src/export-mocks/react-router.ts` to normalize `path/fullPath` by removing pathless group segments (e.g. `/(group)`), while preserving the original route `id` for lookup/override behavior.

- **Behavior preserved where identity matters**
  - Route identity remains file-based (`id` unchanged), so existing id-keyed logic (e.g. route overrides) continues to target the same route nodes.

- **Regression coverage**
  - Added focused tests in `src/export-mocks/react-router.test.ts` for:
    - grouped leaf route (`/(group)/page` → path `/page`)
    - pure group route (`/(group)` → path `/`)
  - Added `code/frameworks/tanstack-react/vitest.config.ts` to run package-local tests consistently.

```ts
// before: id/path/fullPath all used raw file path
createFileRoute('/(group)/page') // path mismatch at runtime

// after: keep id, normalize navigable path
// id: '/(group)/page'
// path/fullPath: '/page'
```

#### Manual testing

- covered by the tests 

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Route paths now normalize group and layout notation, strip extraneous underscore markers, and default empty results to "/" for consistent URLs.
  * Initial route path inference improved to prefer normalized identifiers before falling back to child/full-path or root.

* **Tests**
  * Added tests validating route ID preservation and path/fullPath normalization across grouped and layouted routes.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

issue.title: [BUG]: Docs -> GH action integration is getting flagged by zizmor itself
issue.body:
### Pre-submission checks

- [x] I am **not** filing a feature request. These should be filed via the feature request form instead.
- [x] I have checked the [Troubleshooting Guide](https://docs.zizmor.sh/troubleshooting) for my problem.
- [x] I have looked through both the [open](https://github.com/zizmorcore/zizmor/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+) and [closed](https://github.com/zizmorcore/zizmor/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20state%3Aclosed) issues for a duplicate report.

### zizmor version

1.25.2

### Expected behavior

Hello there :wave: 

First. A huge thank you for this amazing project that helped me a lot and many other OSS projects :) 

https://docs.zizmor.sh/integrations/#manual-integration

the provided snippets shouldn't be flagged by zizmor itself (except if it's on purpose ?)



### Actual behavior

astral-sh/uv seems to enable caching by default and zizmor flag this.



### Reproduction steps

1. use the snippet for manual integration with advanced security
2. zizmor flag its own yml file

### Logs

```plain text

```

### Additional context

_No response_

comment.body: Yes, you probably won't have access to our code scanning but here's a screenshot :
That's the goal. Any of the specified dependencies classifies the project as tanstack-react project
